quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 10 Quiz 6
quiz.subtitle: Force on a Current-Carrying Conductor in a Magnetic Field
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question: A current-carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field experiences:
questions.explanation: When a conductor carrying current is placed in a magnetic field, it experiences a mechanical force. This is the motor effect and is the principle behind electric motors.

questions.question: Which rule determines the direction of force on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field?
questions.explanation: Fleming's left-hand rule: thumb = force (motion), forefinger = magnetic field (N to S), middle finger = current (+ to -). All three are mutually perpendicular.

questions.question: In Fleming's left-hand rule, the forefinger represents:
questions.explanation: Forefinger = Field (North to South). Thumb = Thrust/Force. Middle finger = Current (positive to negative).

questions.question: The force on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field is maximum when the conductor is:
questions.explanation: Force F = BIL sinθ. When θ = 90° (perpendicular), sin90° = 1, so force is maximum. When parallel (θ = 0°), force is zero.

questions.question: The force on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field is zero when the conductor is:
questions.explanation: F = BIL sinθ. When the conductor is parallel to the field (θ = 0°), sin0° = 0, so no force acts.

questions.question: Which of the following does NOT affect the magnitude of force on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field?
options: Length of the conductor in the field (L)
questions.explanation: F = BIL. The force depends on B, I, and L. Resistance does not appear directly in the formula — it may affect current indirectly, but it is not a direct factor.

questions.question: A wire of length 0.5 m carries a current of 4 A and is placed perpendicular to a magnetic field of 0.2 T. What is the force on the wire?
If the current in a conductor is doubled and the magnetic field strength is halved, the force on the conductor (placed perpendicular to the field) will:
questions.explanation: F = BIL. New F' = (B/2) × (2I) × L = BIL = F. The force remains unchanged.

questions.question: The direction of force on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field can be reversed by:
options: Either reversing current or magnetic field direction
questions.explanation: Reversing either the current or the magnetic field direction reverses the force. Reversing both leaves the force direction unchanged.

questions.question: A conductor carrying current experiences no force when placed in a magnetic field. This could be because:
options: The conductor is parallel to the field
The magnetic field is too strong
The conductor is made of copper
questions.explanation: When the conductor is parallel to the magnetic field, θ = 0°, and F = BIL sin0° = 0. No force acts regardless of the current or field strength.

questions.question: The device that converts electrical energy into mechanical energy using the force on a current-carrying conductor is the:
questions.explanation: An electric motor uses the motor effect — the force on a current-carrying coil in a magnetic field — to produce rotation and convert electrical energy into mechanical energy.

questions.question: If a conductor of length 2 m carries 3 A and experiences a force of 12 N when placed perpendicular to a magnetic field, what is the magnetic field strength?

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the force on a current-carrying conductor and Fleming's left-hand rule!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the factors affecting force magnitude and the F = BIL formula.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on Fleming's left-hand rule, the angle dependence (sinθ), and force calculations.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: motor effect, Fleming's left-hand rule (thumb=force, forefinger=field, middle finger=current), and F = BIL.
